top_categories.workout_context: 75 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Valley GranFondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Valley GranFondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Valley GranFondo. Train it until it feels boring.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Base fitness for Valley GranFondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 75 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Valley GranFondo's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Valley GranFondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Valley GranFondo, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.
At Valley GranFondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Valley GranFondo’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 75 miles of mixed terrain in Langley, Fraser Valley, BC, Canada.
